parsed_review.summary: CP00-702 (RP23 External Integrations II, finance-integrations P02 test/evidence/review bridge, risk B, 40 units RP23.P02.M07.S13-RP23.P02.M09.S08) passes hardened read-only review. [Directly inspected] The descriptor is internally consistent and overclaim-free: deliverable distribution (implementation 16 / claude_review 2 / ui 4 / failure_recovery 4 / test 8 / contract 2 / security_audit 4 = 40) and micro-title distribution (Test And Golden Case Set 10 / Hermes Evidence Packet 22 / Claude Review Packet 8 = 40) match FINANCE_INTEGRATIONS_CP702_REQUIREMENTS exactly, and the M07/M08/M09 row titles match the baseline included_units. No-write/no-real-data/runtime-closed boundaries are upheld in source: FINANCE_INTEGRATIONS_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION (registry.js:154) forces all runtime/write/persist/credential/Hermes-receipt flags false and is frozen into every descriptor, Hermes/Claude packet, and handoff via freezeResult; the manifest has zero writes_product_state:true / emits_runtime_receipt:true / runtime_execution:true / real_financial_data_loaded:true, and no real financial data, account numbers, credentials, or secrets appear anywhere (only negation flags and no-leak guard names). The authority boundary is correct and matches the receipt: descriptor authority_boundary sets claude_is_final_approval=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false; the Claude review packet is read_only=true and promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false; Hermes evidence packets are deterministic descriptor artifacts (emits_runtime_receipt=false), not embedded product runtime. The generated contract projection is valid:true throughout (0 valid:false), with latest_projection = FinanceIntegrationsCp702P02TestEvidenceReviewBridgeDescriptor and CP694-CP701 preserved as historical. The manifest honestly reports production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, and pack_level_claude_review=pending_review with empty receipts; no pre-populated raw-output/receipt exists, so this review is the gating evidence. [Recorded evidence, not re-executed under read-only constraints] command-evidence.json records the full validation matrix (closeout-pack-plan/closeout-pack/rp23/external-integrations-ii/weighted/spec/fullplan/global/goal/rp00 validators, git diff --check, node --test, npm test, npm run build) all at exit_code 0, and the readiness auth-preflight passed (exit 0, 1456-byte well-formed JSON, no auth failure/malformed/tool-shaped output). The pack does not claim runtime or enterprise trust, so the remaining human final approval for enterprise trust is the expected out-of-receipt boundary and does not block this CP closeout. No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings.
